title: [LLHD] Dominance issue with local signal
body:
The following input produces a dominance issue in Mem2Reg:
```mlir
// circt-opt --llhd-mem2reg
hw.module @Foo() {
  %0 = llhd.constant_time <0ns, 0d, 1e>
  %c0_i8 = hw.constant 0 : i8
  %c0_i4 = hw.constant 0 : i4
  %c0_i32 = hw.constant 0 : i32
  %1 = hw.bitcast %c0_i8 : (i8) -> !hw.array<2xi4>
  %a = llhd.sig %1 : !hw.array<2xi4>
  %2 = llhd.prb %a : !hw.inout<array<2xi4>>
  llhd.process {
    cf.br ^bb2(%c0_i32 : i32)
  ^bb1:  // pred: ^bb8
    cf.br ^bb2(%c0_i32 : i32)
  ^bb2(%3: i32):  // 3 preds: ^bb0, ^bb1, ^bb7
    %false = hw.constant false
    cf.cond_br %false, ^bb3, ^bb8
  ^bb3:  // pred: ^bb2
    %c0_i2 = hw.constant 0 : i2
    %out = llhd.sig %c0_i4 : i4
    cf.br ^bb4(%c0_i32 : i32)
  ^bb4(%4: i32):  // 2 preds: ^bb3, ^bb5
    cf.cond_br %false, ^bb5, ^bb6
  ^bb5:  // pred: ^bb4
    %5 = llhd.sig.extract %out from %c0_i2 : (!hw.inout<i4>) -> !hw.inout<i1>
    llhd.drv %5, %false after %0 : !hw.inout<i1>
    cf.br ^bb4(%c0_i32 : i32)
  ^bb6:  // pred: ^bb4
    cf.br ^bb7(%c0_i4 : i4)
  ^bb7(%6: i4):  // pred: ^bb6
    cf.br ^bb2(%c0_i32 : i32)
  ^bb8:  // pred: ^bb2
    llhd.wait (%c0_i4, %2 : i4, !hw.array<2xi4>), ^bb1
  }
  hw.output
}
```
Error:
```
broken-dom-reduced-5.mlir:19:14: error: operand #0 does not dominate this use
      %out = llhd.sig %c0_i4 : i4
             ^
broken-dom-reduced-5.mlir:19:14: note: see current operation: %6 = "llhd.prb"(%10) : (!hw.inout<i4>) -> i4
broken-dom-reduced-5.mlir:19:14: note: operand defined here (op in the same region)
```
@mvpant You have been fixing a similar issue recently. Do you know what might be wrong here?
code_blocks:
// circt-opt --llhd-mem2reg
hw.module @Foo() {
  %0 = llhd.constant_time <0ns, 0d, 1e>
  %c0_i8 = hw.constant 0 : i8
  %c0_i4 = hw.constant 0 : i4
  %c0_i32 = hw.constant 0 : i32
  %1 = hw.bitcast %c0_i8 : (i8) -> !hw.array<2xi4>
  %a = llhd.sig %1 : !hw.array<2xi4>
  %2 = llhd.prb %a : !hw.inout<array<2xi4>>
  llhd.process {
    cf.br ^bb2(%c0_i32 : i32)
  ^bb1:  // pred: ^bb8
    cf.br ^bb2(%c0_i32 : i32)
  ^bb2(%3: i32):  // 3 preds: ^bb0, ^bb1, ^bb7
    %false = hw.constant false
    cf.cond_br %false, ^bb3, ^bb8
  ^bb3:  // pred: ^bb2
    %c0_i2 = hw.constant 0 : i2
    %out = llhd.sig %c0_i4 : i4
    cf.br ^bb4(%c0_i32 : i32)
  ^bb4(%4: i32):  // 2 preds: ^bb3, ^bb5
    cf.cond_br %false, ^bb5, ^bb6
  ^bb5:  // pred: ^bb4
    %5 = llhd.sig.extract %out from %c0_i2 : (!hw.inout<i4>) -> !hw.inout<i1>
    llhd.drv %5, %false after %0 : !hw.inout<i1>
    cf.br ^bb4(%c0_i32 : i32)
  ^bb6:  // pred: ^bb4
    cf.br ^bb7(%c0_i4 : i4)
  ^bb7(%6: i4):  // pred: ^bb6
    cf.br ^bb2(%c0_i32 : i32)
  ^bb8:  // pred: ^bb2
    llhd.wait (%c0_i4, %2 : i4, !hw.array<2xi4>), ^bb1
  }
  hw.output
}
broken-dom-reduced-5.mlir:19:14: error: operand #0 does not dominate this use
      %out = llhd.sig %c0_i4 : i4
             ^
broken-dom-reduced-5.mlir:19:14: note: see current operation: %6 = "llhd.prb"(%10) : (!hw.inout<i4>) -> i4
broken-dom-reduced-5.mlir:19:14: note: operand defined here (op in the same region)
